Caption: Time-dependent development of the potential during galvanostatic deposition (−0.15 mA/cm2) for different film deposition times. The indices indicate the total time of electrodeposition of the samples.
(A–F): FE-SEM micrographs of surface; (A) bare zinc coated steel substrate, (B) ED10, (C) ED30, (D) ED60, (E) ED120 and (F) ED300.
SEM micrograph of cross-section of ED300 obtained by FIB-cut.
Raman spectra of the bare zinc coated steel substrate and film coated specimens. The wavelength of the excitation laser was 630 nm.

Left: FT-IRRAS data of the bare zinc coated steel substrate and film coated specimens. A clean gold mirror was used as reference. Right: Evaluation of the OH-stretching regime at ~3500 cm−1 and the metal oxide/hydroxide regime at ~1020 cm−1 as a function of the deposition time.
XPS survey spectra of the bare zinc coated steel substrates, and the mixed hydroxide film deposited for 300 s (ED300).
XPS C 1s and O 1s core level measurements of bare zinc coated steel (Substrate) and after 300 s electrodeposition (ED300).
Bode plots of electrochemical impedance spectroscopy measurements of specimens immersed in borate buffer at pH = 8.3 with addition of 0.05 M NaCl.
